tests.description: parse complete and incomplete SQL statements

steps.description: ordinary word is not a SQLite keyword

tests.description: exercise SQLite LIKE and GLOB pattern helpers
open an in-memory database and step a scalar SELECT statement
tests.notes: Uses SQLite C API pointer-out parameters with typed memory reads/writes, then executes SELECT 40 + 2 against a real :memory: database.

source.description: The sqlite3.wasm binary from @sqlite.org/sqlite-wasm 3.53.0-build1, a package based on the SQLite WebAssembly project and published as an ES module wrapper.

description: SQLite 3.53.0 WebAssembly build from @sqlite.org/sqlite-wasm, exposing the SQLite C API with imported Emscripten memory.
notes: This fixture uses the official @sqlite.org/sqlite-wasm package binary rather than a synthetic SQLite-like wrapper. The tests exercise direct SQLite C API exports, imported env.memory writes, pointer-out reads, a real in-memory SELECT query, SQL statement completeness parsing, keyword lookup, and SQLite LIKE/GLOB helpers without relying on JavaScript glue. The Emscripten openat import is stubbed to report ENOENT for filesystem probes, which still allows SQLite :memory: databases to work.
